---
title: "Explainable Atrial Fibrillation Detection on Single-Lead ECG"
subtitle: "Methods, clinical analyses, and reproducibility notes"
author:
  - name: Laura Piñero Roig
    affiliation: Universitat de Barcelona, Facultat de Medicina
    orcid: 0009-0008-3390-4029
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
---

# Background

Atrial fibrillation (AFib) is the most common sustained arrhythmia and a
leading cause of cardioembolic stroke. Single-lead ECG screening — now
embedded in consumer wearables — has driven a new generation of deep
learning classifiers that match cardiologist-level performance on short
recordings [@hannun2019cardiologist]. Two open problems remain before
such models are clinically deployable:

1. **Explainability.** Models must justify their predictions in a way
   that a clinician can audit at the bedside.
2. **Failure-mode characterisation.** Aggregate metrics hide systematic
   errors on subgroups that a clinician cares about.

This study addresses both, on the publicly available PhysioNet/CinC
Challenge 2017 dataset [@clifford2017af].

# Research question

For a 1-D ResNet trained on the PhysioNet/CinC 2017 single-lead ECG
corpus, does the model

1. **maintain its atrial-fibrillation sensitivity** across stratifying
   variables such as signal quality and record length;
2. **produce Grad-CAM and Integrated-Gradients saliency** that overlaps
   with clinician-annotated regions of interest (absent P waves,
   irregular RR intervals, fibrillatory baseline); and
3. **commit errors organisable into a clinically grounded taxonomy of
   failure modes** (false-negative AFib, atrial flutter mistaken for
   AFib, noise read as rhythm, low-amplitude AF read as sinus) rather
   than appearing as undifferentiated noise in a confusion matrix?

Subgroup audit is a prerequisite for FDA/EMA submission of AI-based ECG
tools; clinician–model concordance is a prerequisite for bedside trust;
a clinical failure-mode taxonomy is what tells the next researcher where
to invest data-collection and model-improvement effort.

The canonical source of this text is
`RESEARCH_QUESTION` in `src/af_explain/evaluation/clinical_analysis.py`.

# Methods

## Data

PhysioNet/CinC Challenge 2017 training set: 8,528 single-lead ECG
recordings, sampled at 300 Hz, 9–60 s long, labelled as Normal,
AFib, Other rhythm, or Noisy. We split stratified 70/15/15 with seed 42.

## Preprocessing

- Zero-phase Butterworth bandpass filter (0.5–40 Hz, order 4) to remove
  baseline wander and high-frequency noise while preserving P, QRS, and T
  morphology.
- Per-record z-score normalisation (mean 0, std 1).
- Fixed-length segmentation to 30 s (9,000 samples). Shorter records are
  symmetrically zero-padded with a mask; longer records are centre-cropped
  (random crop during training).

## Model

A 1-D ResNet with 4 stages of 2 residual blocks each (≈ 18.5 M parameters,
74 MB), kernel size 15 (≈ 50 ms ≈ one QRS width at 300 Hz; odd for
shape-safe stride-2), GroupNorm
(more stable than BatchNorm for long 1-D signals at small per-GPU batch
sizes), and dropout 0.2 inside residual blocks. The architecture follows
[@hannun2019cardiologist].

## Training

- AdamW, learning rate 1e-3, weight decay 1e-4, cosine annealing.
- Class-weighted cross-entropy (inverse frequency).
- Gradient clipping at 1.0.
- Up to 50 epochs, early stopping on validation macro-F1 (patience 10).
- `lightning.Trainer(deterministic=True)`, seed 42.

## Explainability

- **Grad-CAM-1D** [@selvaraju2017gradcam]: backprop the target-class
  logit, weight last-stage activations by GAP-pooled gradients, ReLU,
  upsample linearly to input length.
- **Integrated Gradients** [@sundararajan2017axiomatic]: 50-step Riemann
  sum from a zero baseline to the input, attributions normalised to
  $[-1, 1]$ for plotting.

## Clinical analyses

Four reports are computed on the held-out test set; see
`evaluation/clinical_analysis.py`:

1. **Subgroup robustness.** AFib sensitivity / specificity stratified by
   record-level metadata (signal quality bin, length).
2. **Calibration.** Per-class expected calibration error (ECE) with 15
   bins, reliability diagrams, and a single-parameter temperature
   scaling fit [@guo2017calibration].
3. **Failure-mode taxonomy.** Misclassified records bucketed into
   clinically meaningful categories (false-negative AFib, flutter mistaken
   for AFib, noise-to-rhythm, etc.).
4. **Clinician–model concordance.** For records with expert annotations
   of regions of interest (P waves, RR irregularity windows), we
   compute IoU between Grad-CAM saliency masks and ROI masks plus the
   fraction of top-k saliency peaks lying inside any ROI.

# Results

::: {.callout-note}
Pending the first training run.
:::

# Discussion

::: {.callout-note}
Pending results.
:::

# Reproducibility

```
git clone https://github.com/laurapiro17/af-explain
cd af-explain
uv sync --all-extras --dev
./scripts/download_data.sh
af-train --epochs 50 --seed 42
```

All hyperparameters are persisted in the Lightning checkpoint. CI runs the
test suite on every push.

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
